note_data.title: Ideal gases
keyPoints: An **ideal gas** obeys the equation pV = nRT under all conditions of temperature and pressure
**Kinetic theory** assumes gas molecules are point masses in random motion with elastic collisions
The **pressure** of a gas is caused by the rate of change of momentum when molecules collide with container walls
**Root mean square (rms) speed** is given by c = √(3RT/M) where M is molar mass
**Boltzmann constant** k relates average kinetic energy to absolute temperature: ½mc² = (3/2)kT
Real gases deviate from ideal behavior at **high pressures and low temperatures**
The kinetic theory equation is **pV = (1/3)Nmc²** where N is number of molecules
**Absolute zero** is the temperature at which molecular kinetic energy becomes zero

definitions.definition: A gas that obeys the equation pV = nRT under all conditions and whose molecules have negligible volume and no intermolecular forces
The square root of the mean of the squares of the molecular speeds in a gas sample
The gas constant per molecule, equal to R/NA = 1.38 × 10⁻²³ J K⁻¹
The temperature at which the average kinetic energy of gas molecules is zero, equal to -273°C or 0 K

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Show all algebraic steps clearly, stating any assumptions made
Show working, include units, give answer to appropriate significant figures

modelAnswer.question: Calculate the root mean square speed of nitrogen molecules at 27°C. (Molar mass of nitrogen = 28.0 g mol⁻¹, R = 8.31 J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹)
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Convert temperature to Kelvin: T = 300 K
Mark 2: Substitute values into formula c = √(3RT/M)
Mark 3: Calculate final answer with correct units: 517 m s⁻¹

commonMistakes: Never use Celsius temperature in gas equations — always convert to Kelvin first
Students often confuse molar mass (kg mol⁻¹) with molecular mass — check units carefully
Don't say 'gas molecules have zero volume' — say 'negligible volume compared to container'
Never write pV = nRT for real gases at high pressure — state it applies to ideal gases only
Students mix up c (rms speed) with c̄ (average speed) — they are different quantities
examinerTips: This topic appears almost every year in Paper 4 with calculations
Always show conversion to Kelvin temperature for full marks
Kinetic theory derivations are common — learn the key assumptions
Link molecular kinetic energy to temperature using ½mc² = (3/2)kT
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Molecular motion diagram showing gas molecules as point masses moving randomly in all directions within a cubic container, with arrows indicating elastic collisions with walls
